rows.title_working_draft: Evergrande: the $300B debt math in one chart
rows.notes: Published 2026-05-02 ~03:00 Bangkok to @disclosedch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Eh1_6rRDtTE. Manual fire of v4.6 routine on Friday 2026-05-01 (jumped Monday queue). Editorial gate caught 1 internal-notes error: REVIEW.md said Top Shine bond was '~$862M', actual is HK$862.5M (~US$110M) — error contained in research notes only, never reached narration. 5/5 web-verified claims clean (HCCW 179/2022, Linda Chan, Jan 29 2024, three red lines ratios, Aug 2020, RMB 1.96T H1 2021, Dec 2021 default). Triggered AIVDO v1.8.19 + v1.8.20 — Part 2 stuck-at-21% diagnosed by AIVDO terminal as composition heartbeat bug (Redis-only progress, DB row stale → sweeper false-positive). 3 Part 2 jobs submitted (6e58, bf3ca239, 23faf6fa). First two false-positive swept but actually completed in background (output_url valid). Third (post-v1.8.20) genuinely failed at 30 min — heartbeat fix didn't fire on this code path OR worker actually died AND v1.8.20 disabled the latent Celery-redelivery safety net. Recovery: downloaded 6e58's output_url directly, saved as part2.mp4, ran render.py for local crossfade stitch only. Final mp4 223 MB. Side-finding: OpenAI input-images-per-minute cap of 5/min hit 3/8 scenes with 429 — separate AIVDO handoff candidate. Editorial corrections: e182871 (HKCFI 363 cite + REVIEW.md HK$ fix). YOUTUBE.md description/tags didn't parse via youtube_upload.py — pasted manually in Studio (parser regex bug worth fixing on Monday).

rows.title_working_draft: CP Group: the chicken-feed company that became Asia's quietest conglomerate
rows.notes: SEA experiment #1 added 2026-05-01. Anchor: Dhanin Chearavanont (Forbes 2024 ~$14.4B), CP All exclusive 7-Eleven Thailand franchise since 1989, Lotus's acquired from Tesco 2020 ($10.6B), CP Foods world's largest poultry producer, True+DTAC merger 2023. Angle: vertical integration from soybean feed → poultry → 7-Eleven shelf → telecom touches every Thai consumer dollar. Underexplored on English YouTube. Editorial flag: succession dynamics within Chearavanont family — keep narrative on business mechanics, not personal.

rows.title_working_draft: THAI Airways' bankruptcy and the 4-year debt unwind
rows.notes: SEA experiment #2 added 2026-05-01. Anchor: 2020-05-26 court rehabilitation petition under Thai Bankruptcy Act §90, ฿332B debt at filing, plan approved 2022-06-15, returned to profit FY2023 (~฿28B), exit-from-rehab plan progressed 2024-25. Angle: a flag-carrier that did NOT receive a government bailout — restructured via court process, came back profitable. Underexplored vs. typical 'airline bailout' narrative. Editorial flag: confirm latest exit-from-rehab status at brief time (status moves quarterly).

rows.title_working_draft: LINE Thailand: how Japan's messaging app lost everywhere except here
rows.notes: SEA experiment #3 added 2026-05-01. Anchor: ~50M Thai users (~70% pop., 2024 data), origin in 2011 Fukushima earthquake (NAVER Japan team built it for emergency comms), lost Indonesia (to WhatsApp), Spain (to WhatsApp), India (never landed). Thailand the only major non-Japan stronghold. LINE MAN delivery + LINE PAY → super-app pivot. Angle: hyper-localization that worked here, didn't elsewhere — why? Editorial flag: avoid speculating on cultural reasons; stick to product timing + competitor data.

rows.title_working_draft: Sea Limited: the $200B SEA unicorn that lost 80% in one year
rows.notes: SEA experiment #4 added 2026-05-01. Anchor: NYSE 2017 IPO at $15, peak 2021-10-19 ~$367/share (~$200B mcap), Free Fire banned in India 2022-02-14 (alleged China data concerns via Tencent stake), Sept 2022 layoffs ~7,000 staff, stock bottomed ~$40 late 2022, recovery to $130s by 2025. Angle: how a single regulatory wall (India ban) cut deeper than the broader tech selloff — Free Fire was 32% of digital entertainment GMV. Editorial flag: distinguish 'India banned Free Fire' (verified) from 'India banned Sea over China ties' (alleged/inferred); cite MeitY order specifically.

rows.title_working_draft: Forex-3D: the $1.6B Thai Ponzi that ran on monk endorsements
rows.notes: SEA experiment #5 added 2026-05-01. Anchor: ~9,800 victims, ฿70B+ alleged loss (~$1.9B at 2022 rates), SEC Thailand criminal complaints filed 2022, defendant Apirak Kothi (a.k.a. 'Apirak F3D'), 2018-2021 operating window, monk-celebrity endorsement pattern. Angle: how religious authority + Thai-style trust networks amplified a textbook 'guaranteed FX returns' Ponzi. **EDITORIAL FLAG: Thai legal proceedings ongoing as of brief time. Phrase as 'alleged', 'charged with', 'indicted on' — NEVER 'convicted' until verdict confirmed via official Thai court records. Cross-check via SEC Thailand press releases + Bangkok Post court reporting only.**

rows.title_working_draft: GoTo: Indonesia's $30B merger that disappointed everyone
rows.notes: SEA experiment #6 added 2026-05-01. Anchor: Gojek + Tokopedia merger announced 2021-05-17, IPO 2022-04-11 at Rp 338/share (~$32B mcap), share price ~Rp 60 by late 2023 (~80% drawdown), 1,300 layoffs 2022-11, share buyback program. Angle: SEA's 'answer to Alibaba' that couldn't reconcile two cap tables, two cultures (super-app vs marketplace), and a public market that priced both for hypergrowth they couldn't sustain. Editorial flag: confirm latest share price + buyback status at brief time.

rows.title_working_draft: SCB X: when Thailand's biggest bank tried to become a tech holdco
rows.notes: SEA experiment #7 added 2026-05-01. Anchor: SCB X holding company restructure announced 2021-09-22, Robinhood Thailand zero-fee broker launched 2020-07, Robinhood shutdown announced 2025-01 (~฿200M cumulative loss, sold to Tisco), CardX consumer credit, AISCB JV with AIS, share price ~24% decline 2022-23 vs SET banking index. Angle: a 119-year-old bank that announced 'tech mothership' transformation, killed its consumer brokerage 4 years later, retreated to traditional banking. Editorial flag: SCB is publicly traded SET-listed; stick to SET filings + audited financials.

rows.title_working_draft: Vietjet's $4B IPO and the SEA low-cost carrier squeeze
rows.notes: SEA experiment #8 added 2026-05-01. Anchor: Nguyen Thi Phuong Thao founded Vietjet 2007, first flight 2011, IPO Ho Chi Minh exchange 2017-02-28 (~$1.4B mcap, peak ~$4B), Madame Thao becomes SEA's first female self-made billionaire (Forbes 2017), fleet ~105 aircraft 2024, post-COVID recovery 2023. The 'bikini calendar' PR strategy 2012-2018 — controversial but built awareness against AirAsia/Lion Air on a thin budget. Angle: how an LCC outflanked AirAsia on Vietnamese home turf via aggressive PR + low-cost ops. Editorial flag: cover the bikini-ad strategy as a marketing/PR case-study lens (it's well-documented and Thao herself defended it on the record); avoid editorializing on appropriateness.
